identify_cve.expected_solution: Agent must identify the CVE as CVE-2021-44228 (GHSA-jfh8-c2jp-5v3q) affecting the maven package `org.apache.logging.log4j:log4j-core` at versions 2.0-beta9 - 2.14.1, fixed in 2.15.0 (partial), 2.16.0 (full), backports: 2.3.1, 2.12.2. The BLAST_RADIUS.md report should name the CVE identifier explicitly and the vulnerable version range.
evaluation_criteria: Names the CVE identifier CVE-2021-44228 verbatim
Cites the vulnerable version range 2.0-beta9 - 2.14.1 (and/or fixed version 2.15.0 (partial), 2.16.0 (full), backports: 2.3.1, 2.12.2)

find_direct_deps.expected_solution: Agent must enumerate the direct dependents of `org.apache.logging.log4j:log4j-core` inside the workspace repos. Per ground_truth, these are: spring-boot-starter-log4j2, kafka, and elasticsearch. The answer must reference each by package name and cite the manifest file (e.g. package.json, go.mod, requirements.txt, pom.xml) where the dependency is declared.
evaluation_criteria: Lists each ground-truth direct dependent: spring-boot-starter-log4j2, kafka, and elasticsearch
References the manifest file(s) where the dependency is declared (package.json / go.mod / requirements.txt / pom.xml / Cargo.toml as applicable)
Distinguishes runtime vs dev/test dependencies for `org.apache.logging.log4j:log4j-core` where the manifest type indicates it

trace_transitive_paths.expected_solution: Agent must trace transitive dependency paths through intermediaries. Per ground_truth: log4j-core -> spring-boot-starter-log4j2 -> spring-boot-apps; log4j-core -> kafka-clients -> kafka-streams -> kafka-apps; pax-logging-log4j2 -> OSGi-applications. The dependency_chain detail is: log4j-core -> spring-boot (optional dependency (via spring-boot-starter-log4j2)) via pom.xml / build.gradle; affected_versions=2.0-beta9 - 2.14.1; log4j-core -> apache/kafka (dependency (logging framework)) via build.gradle; affected_versions=2.0-beta9 - 2.14.1; log4j-core -> apache/kafka (also: pax-logging-log4j2 (OSGi)) via pom.xml; affected_versions=shaded copy. The answer must show at least one A -> B -> C path that explains how a downstream consumer pulls in `org.apache.logging.log4j:log4j-core` indirectly.
evaluation_criteria: Documents at least one transitive path of the form A -> B -> C through `org.apache.logging.log4j:log4j-core`
Names every intermediary package present in the ground_truth transitive_paths (kafka-clients, kafka-streams, and spring-boot-starter-log4j2)
Distinguishes direct from transitive dependents in the report

version_analysis.expected_solution: Agent must classify each consumer as affected or unaffected based on the resolved/pinned `org.apache.logging.log4j:log4j-core` version. The vulnerable range is 2.0-beta9 - 2.14.1; the fix landed in 2.15.0 (partial), 2.16.0 (full), backports: 2.3.1, 2.12.2. The answer must explicitly call out the version each consumer pins (from package-lock.json / yarn.lock / go.sum / Pipfile.lock / Cargo.lock as applicable) and decide affected vs not.
evaluation_criteria: States the vulnerable range 2.0-beta9 - 2.14.1 and the fixed version 2.15.0 (partial), 2.16.0 (full), backports: 2.3.1, 2.12.2
Classifies at least one consumer as affected or not affected based on its pinned/resolved version
References a lockfile or resolution manifest as the source of the pinned version
